steps.title: Identify that the minimum occurs at the vertex
steps.content: The function $f(x) = 18x^2 - 45x + 13$ is a quadratic with a positive leading coefficient ($a = 18 > 0$), so the parabola opens upward and has a minimum at its vertex. For any quadratic $f(x) = ax^2 + bx + c$, the x-coordinate of the vertex is given by:

steps.title: Apply the vertex formula with the coefficients
steps.content:
From the function, $a = 18$ and $b = -45$. Substitute into the formula:
$$x = -\frac{(-45)}{2(18)} = \frac{45}{36}$$
Reduce the fraction by dividing by the GCD (9):
$$x = \frac{5}{4} = 1.25$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
